mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, leading to higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions, which reduces consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal shows no predictive power over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and industrial production indicates broad-based economic expansion, which boosts corporate profit expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy in backtests covering at least three distinct business cycles, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation exceeds the federal funds rate, negative real rates trigger capital flight from equities into hard assets, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally persistently despite sustained negative real rates, or the spread between CPI and FEDFUNDS shows zero correlation with forward SPX returns over a 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales figures reflect increasing consumer demand, which directly supports corporate revenue projections and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns show no statistically significant difference between months with rising versus falling retail sales, or the signal's information ratio drops below 0.5 over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production below its long-term baseline indicates supply-side weakness and potential recessionary pressures, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines without corresponding equity drawdowns, or the threshold breach fails to predict negative returns in more than 40% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent job growth and declining unemployment confirm a healthy labor market, which sustains wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces a negative Sharpe ratio over a 5-year walk-forward test, or job growth and unemployment moves diverge without impacting equity valuations.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of accelerating real GDP growth, housing construction activity amplifies the expansionary signal, leading to robust corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth and housing starts move inversely to equity returns during recovery phases, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for interest rate changes.
